title: Phyllorkis auricoma
taxonomy: Phyllorkis auricoma is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This plant belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae, and its genus Phyllorkis is part of the tribe Coelogyneae. The species Phyllorkis auricoma is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its unique floral characteristics. Further classification places Phyllorkis auricoma in the subtribe Coelogyninae. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.

intro: Phyllorkis auricoma, also known as the Golden Ground Orchid, is a species of orchid that is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis auricoma include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin treatments.
